title: Syracuse to show possible changes for streets near I-81, take public feedback
summary: The City of Syracuse is hosting two public open houses (Nov 12-13, 2025) to gather community feedback on the Reconnecting Communities project, which proposes streetscape improvements and a linear park along five South Side corridors affected by I-81 viaduct removal. A $500,000 federal grant funded design consultant Stantec to develop solutions enhancing walkability, biking, and transit connectivity across Colvin, Taylor, MLK, Adams, and Harrison streets.
department_refs: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs

entity_refs.name: SUNY College of Environmental Science and Forestry

political_signal.description: Administration framing I-81 removal as transformative infrastructure opportunity; emphasizing community engagement and equitable mobility improvements across South Side neighborhoods
